subject: Product Management
title: The Feature Factory Trap
description: Learn The Feature Factory Trap in one minute.

hook.text: A 'feature factory' is a product team that measures success by features shipped — not by outcomes achieved.

buildup.text: Feature factories feel productive. The roadmap is full. Engineers are busy. Releases happen on schedule.

discovery.text: But nobody checks if features are used. The product grows complex while core workflows get neglected.

twist.text: Maintenance cost explodes. New features break old ones. Onboarding becomes overwhelming. Users flee to simpler alternatives.

climax.text: The cure: measure outcomes, not output. Ask 'did this feature change behavior?' not 'did we ship on time?'

punchline.text: Shipping features isn't progress. Changing outcomes is.

quiz.question: What defines a 'feature factory' team?
options: Measuring success by features shipped rather than outcomes achieved
A team that builds too few features
A team that only works on bug fixes
